parsed_review.summary: CP00-879 (Risk C, 150 units, RP29.P02.M06.S09-RP29.P03.M04.S11) is a descriptor-only Commercial Readiness bridge: synthetic fixture / test-golden / Hermes-evidence / Claude-review / closeout-handoff rows plus the RP29.P03 interface-contract foundation. Source inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack is genuinely descriptor-only and runtime-closed. states.js/model.js/policies.js/service.js/interface.js/registry.js/validators.js are pure functions returning deep-frozen objects with no writes, no persistence, no runtime receipts, synthetic-only data (tenant-synthetic-*, matter-synthetic-*), and no credentials/secrets. Policy fails closed: denied for missing context and cross-tenant access; review-required for unverified_release/missing_observability/compliance_evidence_gap/unsafe_deploy/customer_plan_mismatch; approval-required for production release. Validators comprehensively enforce the no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime invariants, cross-tenant denial, fail-closed routing, and the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false). The interface contract publishes request/response/error/permission/audit/pagination/serialization/API-fixture metadata only and opens no endpoint; Hermes evidence is metadata-only (emits_runtime_receipt=false) and not embedded in product runtime. model.test.js and service.test.js align with the implementation and exercise happy/denied/review/approval paths, synthetic fixtures, model registry, interface contract, and CP873-CP879 coverage/projection; both are wired into root npm test. The on-disk canonical contract and compatibility alias match the generated projections (validation.valid=true) and all 14 mandatory artifacts exist. Deliverable and micro-title distributions match the pack baseline exactly (150 units; P02=100, P03=50). Manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication all correctly report production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / pending with enterprise trust deferred to human approval - no overclaim. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight returned valid non-empty JSON (not auth failure / 0-byte / malformed), no invalid review attempts are recorded for this pack, source-inspection (not summary-only) mode was used, and only Read/Grep/Glob were permitted. Changed-file scope is respected and unrelated dirty/untracked files (workbook, ldip, progress-control-room, docx) are correctly excluded. One non-blocking P3: a vacuous self-referential mandatory-artifact loop in the descriptor validator (dead code; substantive coverage exists in validateCommercialReadinessContract). No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Final production_ready closeout remains correctly gated on the post-review deterministic validation ladder, exactly as the pack documents.

findings.title: Vacuous self-referential mandatory-artifact check in CP879 descriptor validator
findings.details: In validateCommercialCp879FixtureInterfaceBridgeDescriptor the loop `for (const artifact of requirements.mandatory_artifacts) { if (!requirements.mandatory_artifacts.includes(artifact)) errors.push(...) }` checks the requirements array against itself, so the guard is always false and can never fire. It is dead code that implies a descriptor-level mandatory-artifact validation that does not actually occur. There is no functional gap or safety impact: the substantive mandatory-artifact coverage is enforced in validateCommercialReadinessContract (lines 974-976, against contract.mandatory_artifacts), and all 14 mandatory artifacts are present on disk. The descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, and authority-boundary invariants remain fully enforced.
findings.recommendation: Remove the redundant loop, or repoint it at a meaningful target (e.g. validate descriptor/contract-carried artifacts against requirements.mandatory_artifacts). Non-blocking; safe to defer to a follow-up tidy-up since real coverage exists elsewhere.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking quality note. Does not affect production_ready closeout, the no-write/no-real-data boundary, or the authority boundary. Left to author discretion / future cleanup.
